545.reason: [This condition is incorrect because it assumes any token sequence with '(' two positions ahead is a FuncDef, but Decl can also be followed by tokens that might coincidentally have '(' later. The grammar requires parsing all Decl first, then FuncDef, then MainFuncDef specifically. Also, accessing top(2) may cause index out of bounds when fewer than 3 tokens remain.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition is incorrect because it assumes any token sequence with '(' two positions ahead is a FuncDef, but Decl can also be followed by tokens that might coincidentally have '(' later. The grammar requires parsing all Decl first, then FuncDef, then MainFuncDef specifically. Also, accessing top(2) may cause index out of bounds when fewer than 3 tokens remain.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: checking top(2) assumes at least 3 tokens exist and doesn't properly distinguish between main function, other functions, or declarations with parentheses in expressions. Also fails to handle MainFuncDef which must start with 'int main']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: checking top(2) may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException when fewer than 3 tokens remain, and doesn't correctly distinguish between function definitions and main function based on grammar rules]

172.reason: [The method reads the character after the content of the character literal but doesn't verify it's a closing single quote ('), which means malformed character literals like 'a without closing quote won't be detected as errors]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The method reads the character after the content of the character literal but doesn't verify it's a closing single quote ('), which means malformed character literals like 'a without closing quote won't be detected as errors]

208.reason: [When encountering a single '&', it should create a token with "&" as the value, not "&&". Creating "&&" token for a single '&' character is incorrect and will cause parsing errors.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', it should create a token with "&" as the value, not "&&". Creating "&&" token for a single '&' character is incorrect and will cause parsing errors.]

218.reason: [When encountering a single '|', it should create a token with "|" as the value, not "||". Creating "||" token for a single '|' character is incorrect and will cause parsing errors.]
